title: China launches and commissions 21 major warships in 2025 including aircraft carrier and cruisers
summary: China's People's Liberation Army Navy launched and commissioned at least 21 major combatants in 2025, including an aircraft carrier, amphibious assault ship, five cruisers, and eight additional vessels, marking a significant acceleration in naval modernization.
contents:
# China's PLAN Commissions 21 Major Warships in Record Naval Buildup

## Fleet Expansion Details
China's People's Liberation Army Navy (PLAN) launched and commissioned at least 21 major combatants during 2025, representing one of the most significant naval expansion years in modern Chinese military history.

## Vessel Composition
- 1 aircraft carrier
- 1 amphibious assault ship
- 5 cruisers (likely Type 055 Renhai-class)
- 8 additional major surface combatants
- 6 other commissioned vessels

## Expert Assessment
Asia security expert Bates Gill notes the expansion rate reflects China's strategic commitment to naval dominance in the Western Pacific and beyond. The shipbuilding pace far outstrips any other navy globally, including the US Navy.

## Strategic Implications
- Enhances China's power projection capabilities across the Indo-Pacific
- Strengthens anti-access/area denial (A2/AD) posture around Taiwan
- Increases pressure on regional navies (Japan, Philippines, Australia)
- Affects US strategic calculations for force posture in the Pacific
- Drives allied responses including AUKUS submarine cooperation

## Regional Reactions
Japan, Australia, and other Indo-Pacific nations accelerated their own naval modernization programs in response. The expansion directly influences Japan's defense posture and AUKUS timeline pressures.

## Tags
china, plan, naval-expansion, warships, aircraft-carrier, indo-pacific, military-modernization
